observations.recommendation:
1. Investigate and resolve the cause of the non-conduct of the physical count of PPE, which eventually resulted in the non-preparation and non-submission of the RPCPPE despite the creation of the Inventory Committee.
Accordingly, furnish the Audit Team a copy of the report, once available, pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs for evaluation.
2. Ensure that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers/PPE ledger cards and depreciation schedule for each item of PPE to substantiate the balances presented in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

3. Coordinate with the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF pertaining to the QRF and MOOE from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

4. Reconcile their balances of Cash in Local Treasury as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and conduct the reconciliation regularly in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments and consistently harmonize the balances at year-end.

5. Reconcile their balances of Cash in Bank account as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments to present accurate cash in bank balances at year-end; and
6. Fast-track the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements to substantiate the cash in bank balance reported in the year-end financial statements.

7. Revisit the contract with the Municipality’s systems provider and conduct a review of the employed accounting system if it can really comply with the reportorial requirements of the Commission on Audit and other oversight agencies of the LGU. If not, we further recommended and Management agreed to the use of the enhanced Electronic New Government Accounting System (eNGAS) developed by COA which was encouraged to be used by all government agencies under COA Circular No. 2004-007 dated September 14, 2004.

8. Prepare the following adjusting entries:
Particulars	General Fund Books	Trust Fund Books
Dr. Due from Other Funds – Purchase of Rescue Vehicle	₱900,000.00	₱900,000.00
Cr. Government Equity – Due to Other Funds	₱900,000.00	₱900,000.00
Payment of Insurance
Dr. Due from Other Funds - Insurance Premium	₱402,104.49	₱402,104.49
Cr. Prior Period Adjustment - Due to Other Funds	₱402,104.49	₱402,104.49
9. Facilitate the payment of the Trust Fund to the General Fund to replenish the latter.

11. Ensure that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF pertaining to the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2023 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

15. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the RPTAR or Taxpayer’s Index Cards maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, thereafter, furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy to serve as basis in recording the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year.
16. The Municipal Accountant and the Assistant Municipal Treasurer agreed to reconcile the RPT and SET receivable and the list of delinquent taxpayers at the end of each year to ensure uniformity and consistency in the reports.

17. Demand the immediate liquidation of the cash advances especially those from prior years. Henceforth, we recommended and Management also agreed to strictly follow the prescribed guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

18. Make a written representation to the DOH to determine the unit cost of the unused 759 vials of vaccines. Subsequently, furnish a copy of the unused COVID-19 vaccine report to the Accounting Office for the latter to further verify and thereafter take-up the verified balance in the Municipality’s books of accounts to adjust the ending balances of both the inventory and donation accounts.
19. Both offices observe proper coordination in order to prevent similar omissions/errors in the future operations considering that the vaccination is still a continued priority program in 2023.

20. Stop the practice of recording the purchase of supplies and materials as outright expenses and use the Inventory account method of accounting the inventories as prescribed in IPSAS 12.
21. Reconcile the affected accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances reflected in the financial statements.
22. Prepare the supply ledger cards and supply cards, respectively, and reconcile both records regularly.

23. Coordinate with the concerned offices for the reconciliation of inventory accounts in order to present accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.
24. For inventory items already turned-over to various non-government organizations/associations, we further recommended that Management request from the recipient associations/organizations for the copy of the Certificates of Turnover and Acceptance, if there is any, for the proper recording of these issuances in the books of accounts.

25. Submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the Build and Design Scheme, for evaluation by the Audit Team, and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned Terms of Reference.

26. Submit a valid and special license of the awarded joint venture contractor to regularize these procurement projects. Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure that eligible contractors have acquired the necessary licenses before awarding the contracts.

27. Fill-up the three mandatory positions who are responsible for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning as required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
28. If budgetary constraints exist, we recommended and Management also agreed in considering the options provided under the JMC to comply with the mandatory positions in the establishment of the LDRRM Office.

29. Revisit its contract with the Municipality’s systems provider and conduct a review of the employed accounting system if it can really answer to the reportorial requirements of the Commission on Audit and other oversight agencies of the LGU.
30. If not, we further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the use of the enhanced Electronic New Government Accounting System (eNGAS) developed by COA which was encouraged to be used by all government agencies under COA Circular No. 2004-007 dated September 14, 2004.
31. Obtain the Sangguniang Bayan post-approval of projects already implemented to regularize the transactions in order to avoid audit disallowance.

32. Submit the supporting documents to substantiate each claim and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to set up the accounts payable for each claim against the Municipality in accordance with IPSAS 1.
33. Stop the practice of recording payable accounts in lump sum.
34. Send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the illegally received assistance and ensure that these illegally disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.

35. Require the officials of the associations responsible for projects to submit an accounting of its operation and return the unutilized balance of the fund as well as all the equipment purchased out of the fund.
36. Require the concerned members of the BAC and TWG to refund the amount of honoraria received which was disallowed in audit.
37. Direct the persons responsible to settle immediately the amount suspended in audit amounting to ₱35,520.00 for the grant of monthly TEV of the health personnel of the Municipality.
38. Direct the persons liable to settle immediately the amount disallowed in audit amounting to ₱177,000.00 for the payment of honoraria to the members of the MCPC for CY 2015.
39. Refund the excess payment of their RATA.

40. Verify whether the PUFA Inc is still operating. If it is, it should make a final demand and assess whether legal action for its failure to settle its obligation can be made.
41. Prepare a final demand for payment from the loan borrowers for the hog raising project. A payment scheme should be devised that would enable the borrowers to pay their obligations. At the same time, an evaluation of the liability of the selection committee who approved the selection of the borrowers and the office responsible in the monitoring of the implementation of the project as these individuals contributed to its failure should be conducted to discourage laxity in implementing projects.
42. Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust prior year’s erroneous recording of the loan principal, receipt of the interest and penalty payments to present fairly the loan receivable account in the financial statements.
43. Renew its website subscription and PhilGEPS website subscription to comply with the posting requirements and promote transparency in its procurement process.

44. Comply with the mandatory posting and submission of the APP and PMR to the LGU’s website and to the GPPB, respectively, to promote transparency and public monitoring of the Municipality’s procurement process.

45. Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement in a timely manner in compliance with the rules and regulations to properly substantiate the Cash in Bank balance reflected in the accounting records.

46. Take appropriate actions to cause for the immediate resolution of these deficiencies.
47. Enforce controls so as to prevent the incurrence of dormant account balances in the financial statements.

48. Establish an Internal Audit Service Office with sufficient support from the top management to gain cooperation/confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.

49. Submit legal basis to support the disbursement / grant of Calamity Assistance to the officials, employees and job orders in the total amount of ₱432,300.00 to prevent disallowance in audit.
50. Submit the list of financial assistance received from other LGUs utilized for the grant of the said calamity assistance.
51. Submit copy of loan Agreement between the municipal mayor and the president of the fishermen’s organization.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Inventory
Committee, to conduct annual physical count and submit the Report on
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) on or before
January 31 of each year. We also recommended and Management agreed to
require the concerned Offices to maintain the Property Cards and PPE Ledger
Cards and fast track the reconciliation of the previous year’s RPCPPE with
the Accounting records.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, to investigate and identify the nature of these variances as well as
prepare necessary adjusting journal entries once the cause of the variance is
identified.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an
explanation/justification on the said disbursement without appropriation, for
the Audit Team’s evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the legal basis on the
said transaction. Otherwise, submit a written explanation/justification on the
above-noted findings, for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal
Accountant, to prepare and submit the monthly BRS in accordance with COA
Circular 96 – 011.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Secure a copy of the guidelines mentioned by the MLGOO on not requiring
the authorization of the DILG in the conduct of the benchmarking activity
if done within the same province with the LGU;
b. Submit proof that the benchmarking activity is included in the CapDev
Agenda, otherwise submit a written explanation why this was conducted
despite its absence in the approved CYs 2023-2025 CapDev Agenda; and
c. Adhere to the rules and regulations in the conduct of study tours or
“lakbay aral” prescribed under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Submit written justification on the noted deficiencies; and
b. Practice prudence in the disbursement of government funds and avoid
unnecessary expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-003.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Submit written justification why further authorization from the SB were
not secured in the current year’s PPAs in the implementation of programs
and projects under lump-sum appropriations in the 20 per cent DF despite
the issuance of the same observation in the previous year; and
b. Secure authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan for the above-
mentioned projects implemented under lump-sum appropriation and
submit to the Audit Team for verification or a written explanation why the
same could not be submitted.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Use the 20 per cent DF to set aside funds for loan obligations based on the
loan amortization schedule provided by the banks to ensure reliable
estimates of payments to be made; and
b. Ensure that the development projects funded out of the 20 per cent DF are
well planned.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Submit written justification on why the delegation of authority to the
Municipal Administrator was not concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan
and obtain the concurrence for CY 2024 and subsequent years; and
b. Review and correct the delegated authority of the Municipal Administrator
in accordance with the National Guidelines on Internal Control System.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Submit written justification on the significant delay of the implementation
of the YPHP and provide proof that the calibrated actions in response to
delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects provided in GPPB
Circular 03-2019 dated March 8, 2019 were adopted;
b. Ensure that liquidated damages will be deducted from the payment to the
contractors, if warranted; and
c. Expedite the completion of the project and deliver the completed housing
units to the identified beneficiaries as soon as possible.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal officials
to allocate at least 5 per cent of their total annual appropriation for GAD
programs/projects/activities in compliance with Section 36 of R.A. 9710.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the BAC, to submit a
written explanation/justification on declaring the supplier as LCRB despite the
negative NFCC and non-submission of line of credit, in lieu of the NFCC
computation.

10. Prioritize the preparation of the CDP 2023-2028, duly harmonized with the approved RDP and PDP 2023-2028, respectively, and its approval thereafter for the effective and efficient implementation of the priority development projects of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Investigate the cause/s of the non-conduct of the physical count of PPE and undertake measures to eventually prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for evaluation; and
Ensure that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers/PPE ledger cards and depreciation schedule for each item of PPE to substantiate the balances presented in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to coordinate with the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF pertaining to the QRF and MOOE from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant agreed to reconcile their respective records of Cash in Local Treasury as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and conduct the subsequent reconciliation regularly in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments and consistently harmonize the balances at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their respective records of Cash in Bank account as of December 31, 2023 and submit the results of reconciliation to the Audit Team for review and effect the necessary corrections and/or adjustments to present accurate cash in bank balances at year-end; and
Municipal Accountant fast-track the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements to substantiate the cash in bank balance reported in the year-end financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revisit the contract with the Municipality’s systems provider and conduct a review of the employed accounting system if it can really comply with the reportorial requirements of the Commission on Audit and other oversight agencies of the LGU. If not, we further recommended and Management agreed to the use of the enhanced Electronic New Government Accounting System (eNGAS) developed by COA which was encouraged to be used by all government agencies under COA Circular No. 2004-007 dated September 14, 2004.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted above:
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant facilitate the payment of the Trust Fund to the General Fund to replenish the latter fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), agreed to prioritize the preparation of the CDP 2023-2028, duly harmonized with the approved RDP and PDP 2023-2028, respectively, and its approval thereafter for the effective and efficient implementation of the priority development projects of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF pertaining to the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2023 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to timely submit the Quarterly Report on PPAs with complete information on or before the 10th day of the month following the end of each quarter, otherwise, impose administrative disciplinary action to the responsible officials every time the reports are not submitted within the required time.
We recommended and the BAC, through the Secretariat, agreed to comply with GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 and furnish the Audit Team with proof of submission of the prescribed reports to the GPPB.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and other concerned LGU officials agreed to impose warranty security in the procurement of goods in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184, otherwise, any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period shall be charged to the concerned approving official.

Review and reconcile the transactions recorded under the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries, if necessary, to present a reliable balance in the financial statements in accordance with IPSAS 1.

Implement projects that are consistent with the approved LDIP and with the RDP and PDP so as to synchronize the actions of the LGU with that of the regional and national levels in view of improving the welfare of its constituents.
Provide a written explanation on charging the subject expenditures to LDRRMF instead of General Fund.
Strictly comply with the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances on LDRRMF utilization.
The LDRRM Fund be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs.
Stop the practice of granting cash advance to any elected officials for purposes other than what is allowed under Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Prepare and submit the monthly BRS as of December 31, 2022 to substantiate the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance reflected in the year-end financial statements.
Submit an acceptable explanation/justification or its legal basis on the noted deficiency, for the Audit Team’s evaluation to avoid suspension or disallowance.

Strict compliance with the guidelines on the utilization of the DRRM Fund, ensuring that only programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery are implemented.

Deduct the ten percent (10%) retention money from each progress payment for infrastructure projects up to fifty percent (50%) completion of works, if the project is within schedule otherwise, it should be continuously imposed.
Submit an acceptable explanation/justification on why warranty security was not required in the procurement of goods.

Adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 to ensure that warranty securities are required in future procurements of goods, otherwise, any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period shall be charged to the concerned approving official.

Refrain from indicating generic or lump-sum appropriation in the Annual/Supplemental Budget as well as the Annual/Supplemental Investment Program, and henceforth adhere to the provisions of Article 454 (d) of the Rules and Regulation Implementing Republic Act No. 7160.

Carry out sufficient planning in determining the PPAs to be funded under the 20% Development Fund and include only those that are specifically described/determined and the project is likewise implementation-ready.

The BAC and the BAC Secretariat to revisit the guidelines in the preparation of the APP as prescribed in GPPB Circular 07-2015 and make sure that all procurement activities of the LGU, including infrastructure projects, are incorporated in the APP in accordance with RA 9184 so that proper procurement planning could be achieved.
Observe strictly the provisions of DBM BC No. 2007-3 in the grant of honoraria for the members of the BAC and TWG to avoid disallowance.

Refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time that the PS cap/limitation shall have been observed pursuant to the provisions in LBC No. 75 dated July 12, 2002, and LBC No. 84 dated April 13, 2007.

Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the monthly trial balance, its supporting schedules of general and subsidiary ledgers and Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, to substantiate the balances in the financial statements.
Review the personnel complement and ensure that offices are manned by sufficient and competent personnel to meet deadlines and submit complete reports.
Provide cut-off in the processing of payments for the year to expedite the recording of the transactions and the closing of books of the LGU.
Review the practices of the BAC Secretariat vis-à-vis its tasks and functions as stipulated in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.
Submit distribution list for the relief goods distributed to constituents and ensure that control procedures and complete documentation are established for claims against government funds.
Submit the lacking supporting documents on the purchase of radio communications equipment and signal enhancers for the Audit Team’s evaluation.
Submit proof that Rescue Reconnaissance I is an accredited training organization by the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council.

Include an appropriation for the settlement of the long-term liabilities of the agency so that payment of its overdue loan amortizations could be made possible. The Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entry to take-up interest due in the books.
We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned reports to avoid disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate require the
concerned  accountable  officers  to  immediately  settle  their  cash
advances, otherwise consider the imposition of sanction as provided in
Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the  withholding  of  salary.
Further, refrain from granting additional cash advance unless  the
previous one has been liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take immediate  action  to  fill  up
the position of the Municipal Accountant with qualified  personnel  so  that
efficiency and effectiveness in  the  discharge  of  the  functions  can  be
attained.  Further, observe the rules  and  regulations  on  designation  as
mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate review and  analyze
the transactions pertaining to the affected assets  and  liability  accounts
with  negative  balances  and  effect  the  necessary  adjustments  so  that
reliable balances of  the  accounts  will  be  presented  in  the  financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate  exert  efforts  to
reconcile the reciprocal accounts for all funds by  identifying  the  causes
of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the  books  for
accurate and fair presentation of the same in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management submit the  aforementioned  reports  to
avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee members assist and  mentor
the BAC Secretariat in the preparation and submission of the APP and PMR  to
the GPPB to ensure compliance with RA 9184.  Further, ensure  that  the  BAC
Secretariat be equipped with relevant and necessary  training  by  attending
seminar on RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  –  Designate  coordinate  with
the Municipal Treasurer  and  the  personnel  in-charge  of  the  Waterworks
System to determine the amount of receivables at the year-end  and  prepared
the Journal Entry Voucher to record the same in the  books  to  reflect  the
actual income derived from the operation.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  –  Designate  reclassify  the
current portion of Loans Payable – Domestic for the proper  presentation  of
the account in the Statement of Financial Position of  the  Municipality  at
year-end.

We recommended that Management submit proof that Rescue Reconnaisance  I  is
an accredited training organization by the National Disaster Risk  Reduction
and Management Council.

The agency should: a) verify whether the PUFA Inc is still operating. If it is, it should make a final demand and assess whether legal action for its failure to settle its obligation can be made. b) prepare a final demand for payment from the loan borrowers for the hog raising project. A payment scheme should be devised that would enable the borrowers to pay their obligations. At the same time, an evaluation of the liability of the selection committee who approved the selection of the borrowers and the office responsible in the monitoring of the implementation of the project as these individuals contributed to its failure should be conducted to discourage laxity in implementing projects. c) require the Municipal Accountant to adjust prior year's erroneous recording of the loan principal, receipt of the interest and penalty payments to present fairly the loan receivable account in the financial statements.

During the exit conference, it was known that the physical inventory of PPE was not conducted for CY 2016 and the reconciliation with the accounting record was not completed  

Report on the Physical count of inventory per office was submitted but not reconciled with the amount of PPE per books.   Also the agency failed to maintain the subsidiary ledger / cards per item of the PPE.   As of December 31, 2015 PPE account had a balance of P102,099,966.65 while the physical inventory had a balance of P40,706,750.54 or a difference of P31,393,216.11

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management notify the public through signboards of their projects at the project location and adhere to the other provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 | The municipality has provided notification signboard on the various projects but not the prescribed form.
observations.reason: Inquiry from the CE Aide Draftsman disclosed that the notification signboard as required in COA Circular 2013-004 was not applied by the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to verify the Due From and Due to Other Funds and other affected accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 2010 | In the process of reconciling the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit legal basis to support the disbursement / grant of Calamity Assistance to the officials, employees and job orders in the total amount of P432,300.00 to prevent disallowance in audit. Also, submit the list of financial assistance received from other LGUs utilized for the grant of the said calamity assistance.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | Management justified that the employees were also victims of the calamity thus they were given assistance.

observations.recommendation: In view thereof, we recommend management to submit proof of accreditation of the Portician Circle of Learning Consultancy Services with the GPPB to conduct the seminar on Strategic Trends in Government Procurement otherwise, the claim in the amount of P119,350.00 will be disallowed in audit.  Further, refrain from sending personnel to attend seminars in the future sponsored by private institution without accreditation.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | Management commits to submit the required proof of accreditation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to verify and reconcile the amount payable to BIR and thereafter remit the same to BIR.  Henceforth, we recommend prompt and regular remittance of all taxes withheld within the prescribed time to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 2012 | The municipality has remitted the taxes withheld but not the intact.
observations.reason: Records showed that the taxes withheld were not remitted intact monthly. As of December 2015, Due to BIR had the following balances: GF – P733,935.51; SEF – P15,470.32 and TF – (P139,787.45).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should direct the concerned Municipal Accountant to submit to the concerned Auditor those lacking documents of the subject post-audited transactions to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.  Henceforth, the said Municipal Accountant should always see to it that the DVs covering claims against government funds are supported with complete documents pursuant to Section 4 of P. D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 | Management is in the process of collating all the documents for submission to COA
observations.reason: Supporting documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the concerned Accountant to:

Refrain from setting up accounts payable unless the obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and unless there is authority for its incurrence pursuant to Section 46 of P. D. No. 1177; and

Conduct a review of the composition of the Accounts Payable account to determine their validity and effect the necessary accounting entries to revert all undocumented payables.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 | The account has been in the books for quite a long time without movement and the composition of which is difficult to identify.

observations.recommendation: We require the Accountant/Accounting Section to conduct a thorough verification of the abnormal balances recorded in the books of accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments to clean the books of this deficiency.  We likewise reiterated our recommendation for a thorough review of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and source documents before recording the transactions in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2013
2011 2003 | The Municipal Accountant did not made an in-depth analysis/adjustment for these abnormal accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant informed that these abnormal accounts are to be adjusted in 2015 but per verification, the same remained unacted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of P1,955,445.81 pursuant to Item 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 | As of December 2015, the Municipal Treasurer did not transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of P1,964,003.49 to the special trust fund.
observations.reason: As of yearend, the municipality did not transfer the balance of the LDRRM fund to a separate special trust fund account.

observations.recommendation:
The agency should: 
a)  verify whether the PUFA Inc is still operating.  If it is, it should make a final demand and assess whether legal action for its failure to settle its obligation can be made.   
b) prepare a final demand for payment from the loan borrowers for the hog raising project.  A payment scheme should be devised that would enable the borrowers to pay their obligations.  At the same time, an evaluation of the liability of the selection committee who approved the selection of the borrowers and the office responsible in the monitoring of the implementation of the project as these individuals contributed to its failure should be conducted to discourage laxity in implementing projects.   
c) require the Municipal Accountant to adjust prior years erroneous recording of the loan principal, receipt of the interest and penalty payments to present fairly the loan receivable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2010
2007 | No action taken by management with regards to the loans granted to PAFU Inc. as well as to individuals for the Small and Medium Enterprise Development Program, thus remained uncollected
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant informed they have difficulty in collecting the payments for the loan since most of the projects undertaken by the loan grantees were unsuccessful.

observations.recommendation: The Sangguniang Bayan should include an appropriation for the settlement of the long-term liabilities of the agency so that payment of its overdue loan amortizations could be made possible. The Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entry to take-up interest due in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 | The Municipality communicated with the Office of the President requesting for the condonation of interest.
observations.reason: Per letter from the Office of the President dated October 10, 2008, the municipality was informed that Pres. Arroyo has directed to treat the program fund as non-interest bearing seed capital.  However, per verification with the Mun. Accountant, the municipality has failed to pay the principal amount of P1M.

observations.recommendation: Submit copy of loan agreement between the municipal mayor and the president of the fishermen’s organization.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2004 | The Municipality was demanding copy of the loan agreement from the Municipal Agriculturist.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still waiting for a word from the Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized to fund priority programs or projects only which are capital in nature in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the officials of the associations responsible for projects to submit an accounting of its operation and return the unutilized balance of the fund as well as all the equipment purchased out of the fund.

We recommend that Management institute policies on the grant of cellular allowances that is considered economical and will suit the needs of the Municipality and be guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We also recommend that Management justify the giving of additional cellular allowances to the members of the BAC, Secretariat and TWG based on their different functions as mandated by R.A. 9184.
We recommend that the BAC designate members of the TWG who have technical expertise that will suit to the nature of each procurement of the Municipality.

We recommend that the LDRRMO submit the required monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to the COA Auditor within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
We recommend that the persons responsible for the procurement immediately comply the requirements of the Notice of Suspension to avoid the disallowance of the questioned transactions.

We recommend that Management observe strictly the provisions of DBM BC No. 2007-3 in the grant of honoraria for the members of the BAC and TWG to avoid disallowance.  Further, require the concerned members of the BAC and TWG to refund the amount of honoraria received which was disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit immediately a letter of explanation duly supported with proofs that the items were withdrawn by the supplier in compliance with the recommendation of the Inspection Team from the DILG and also submit the complete list / inventory of materials returned as replacement as well as the items stored at the municipal bodega for reinspection.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the Inspection Report from the DILG as proof that the items were with withdrawn by the supplier in compliance with the recommendation by the Inspection Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment by proper management of solid wastes. The LGU officials should facilitate the closure of the open dumpsite and implement proper handling of solid wastes as authorized under the law.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that presently, they are operating a controlled dumpsite since the volume of garbage collected is manageable and they cater only to few barangays.  Also, they have filed a request for closure of the dumpsite and the MRF facility construction is soon to be started.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management comply with the policy on GAD budget and all applicable provisions in RA No. 9710 otherwise known as the Magna Carta for Women, and ensure full implementation of all projects, programs and activities included in the GAD plan to achieve the overall goals of gender and development.

We recommend the concerned Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the provision of the Joint Circular 2015-01 in the grant of cash advance for confidential and/or intelligence fund and submit written explanation on the failure to submit the cash advance vouchers within seven (7) days after release of check.
observations.managementAction: Starting CY 2016, no more cash advance for Confidential Fund was granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to hasten the completion of the unfinished projects in order that the constituents can enjoy the benefits to be derived therefrom.

We recommend that management:

Establish GAD Focal Point System to spearhead the preparation of the agency annual performance-based GAD Plans, Programs and Budgets in response to gender issues of their constituents and clients and in the context of the agency mandate, among other functions,  in accordance with PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01; and

Prepare and submit GPB and GAD AR approved by the agency head within the reglementary period and refrain from charging to GAD budget projects not supportive of gender issues as prescribed under Joint Circular 2012-01 of PCW, NEDA and DBM.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013 | The LCE appointed the ABC President as the GAD Focal Person | The GPB and the AR was submitted with indorsement from DILG.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management the following:

Require the Accountant to book up the receivable of P1,110,523.50 to correct the understatement of income account for waterworks. 

Intensify collection of receivable by requiring the collectors to meet certain targets.  

Require the collectors to submit monthly report of receivables to be used as basis for the recording of receivables in the books.

Review and investigate causes for the non collection of monthly dues and adopt measures to correct them.

Enforce disconnection of delinquent consumers if warranted
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling the accounts based on the updated schedules / lists of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management initiate administrative and legal sanctions against the contractor and forfeit the corresponding performance bond in favor of the municipality for contract violations.
observations.reason: The LGU has terminated the contract but no action with regards to the violations of the contract which caused delay in the completion of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the persons responsible to settle immediately the amount suspended in audit amounting to P35,520.00 for the grant of monthly TEV of the health personnel of the municipality.
observations.reason: No appeal was made by the concerned employees. The Accountant informed that some employees have already refunded the amount claimed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant – Designate to update and prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statements and prepared the necessary adjusting entries affecting the accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | Bank Reconciliation for the three LBP accounts was already prepared except for the General Fund.
observations.reason: All accounts except for the General Fund maintained at LBP and DBP were prepared.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommend the following:

Management to require the association to submit status report on the operation of its Banana Chips Manufacturing together with the financial statements and inventory verified by the Municipal Accountant – Designate and inform the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) of the status thereof.

Assess the operation of AWA to ensure that the fund entrusted to the association for the implementation of the project has been fully utilized and accounted for including the evaluation of the members’ commitment and willingness to help sustain the business.      

If the operation was found to be not anymore feasible, require the termination of the project and seek assistance / advice from DOLE on the possibility of utilizing the fund to finance other viable project.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 | Status report on the operation of the association was submitted but no communication or report was submitted to DOLE for the appropriate action since the production of banana chips was stopped.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the association was inoperative but not report was submitted to DOLE on the status of the operation of the said association.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact all her collections to safeguard the funds from possible loss or misappropriation and strictly comply Sec 32 of COA Circular 92-382.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 | Deposit of collections was made twice a month.
observations.reason: Matching of collections and deposits showed that remittances made to the AGDB were made twice a month.

observations.recommendation: Concerned officials should be required to refund the excess payment of their RATA.  Furthermore, the management should refrain from implementing the new RATA rates until such time that the PS cap/limitation shall have been observed pursuant to the provisions in LBC No. 75 dated July 12, 2002, and LBC No. 84 dated April 13, 2007.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2007 | Some officials have already refunded the disallowed RATA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accountant to require all concerned officers and employees for the immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2013.  Otherwise, the concerned Local Chief Executive should advice in writing the concerned Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to suspend the salaries of concerned accountable officers and employees to compel the settlement of their respective outstanding accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2013; 2012 | Unliquidated cash advances were deducted from the salaries of the concerned employees.
observations.reason: As of Dec. 2016, schedule of cash advances showed that some cash advances were already liquidated.
